title: Phytohormone Profiling by Liquid Chromatography Coupled to Mass Spectrometry
author: Camilo E. Vital
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zgff3tn
materials: The mobile phase consists of buffers A (water acetic acid 0.02%) and B (acetonitrile acetic acid 0.02%) and a gradient of %B: 5% x 0 min­ -1 ; 60% x 11 min­ -1 ; 95% x 13 min­ -1 ; 95% x 17 min­ -1 ; 5% x 19 min­ -1 ; and 5% x 20 min­ -1 . The solvent flow rate is 0.3ml min­ -1 in a column at 30ºC.

steps.title: Collect samples of plant tissues, immediately...
steps.instruction: Collect samples of plant tissues, immediately freeze in liquid nitrogen and store them in freezer -80°C until use.

steps.title: Macerate the samples in liquid nitrogen using mortar and pestle
steps.instruction: Macerate the samples in liquid nitrogen using mortar and pestle. Do not allow to thaw. Weigh approximately 110mg of each sample into microtubes (2ml) and annotate the weight (used for absolute quantifications). Alternatively, the samples may be weighed into 1.5 ml tubes (eppendorff - round bottom) and macerate using glass or tungsten beads at 25 Hz / s for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Add 400μl of a solution containing methanol:isopropanol:acetic acid (20:79:1)
steps.instruction: Add 400μl of a solution containing methanol:isopropanol:acetic acid (20:79:1).

steps.title: Vortex samples 4 times for 20...
steps.instruction: Vortex samples 4 times for 20 seconds (keep on ice) and then sonicate for 10 minutes. Let the samples stand on ice for 30 minutes and again sonicate for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 13000g for 10 minutes...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 13000g for 10 minutes at 4ºC and collect the supernatants in new tubes.

steps.title: To the remaining pellet, repeat the...
steps.instruction: To the remaining pellet, repeat the procedures 3, 4 and 5 (supernatant

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples a 20000g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples a 20000g for 5 minutes at 4ºC to remove debris and collect about 600μl for new tubes

steps.title: Filter the supernatant using disposable 0
steps.instruction: Filter the supernatant using disposable 0.2ml PVDF membrane.

steps.title: Store the samples in freezer -80ºC...
steps.instruction: Store the samples in freezer -80ºC until analyze them using LC-MS.

steps.title: Prepare a standard solution containing 1
steps.instruction: Prepare a standard solution containing 1.0 ug/mL of each compound in methanol:isopropanol:acetic acid (20:79:

steps.title: Setup a product ion scan method...
steps.instruction: Setup a product ion scan method for each compound and optimize the transmission and fragmentation parameters. This procedure may be executed manually or automatically. A second transition can be used to confirm the first transition used for the quantification, especially for low intensity chromatogram signals.

steps.title: Select the higher intensity fragment ions...
steps.instruction: Select the higher intensity fragment ions to compose the transition list used in the scan mode by MRM (Multiple Reaction Monitoring) as illustrated in the Table 1. %s %s

steps.title: Prepare serial dilutions of 0
steps.instruction: Prepare serial dilutions of 0.1 ng/mL up to 400 ng/mL in according with the mass spectrometer sensitivity and linearity. Two replicate is enough to prepare the standard curves.

steps.title: Inject 5
steps.instruction: Inject 5.0 ul and perform the MRM method as setup in

steps.title: Generate the area from XICs (extracted...
steps.instruction: Generate the area from XICs (extracted ion chromatograms) for each dilution using Skyline software. Note: A complete tutorial for processing of the mass spectra data using Skyline software is described in the Supplementary Material

steps.title: Export the XICs to Microsoft Excel...
steps.instruction: Export the XICs to Microsoft Excel following the instructions of the Skyline tutorial (Supplementary Material 1).

steps.title: Prepare the standard curves for each...
steps.instruction: Prepare the standard curves for each compound in ng/mL of fresh tissue. Use the XIC area versus phytohormones concentration (ng/mL) to generated linear curves (Figure 1). Figure

steps.title: Schematic diagram showing the steps to...
steps.instruction: Schematic diagram showing the steps to prepare the standard curves and to final quantification of phytohormones

steps.title: UHPLC system containing vials for 1mL...
steps.instruction: UHPLC system containing vials for 1mL and loop for injection of 5μl.

steps.title: Use a mass spectrometer triple quadrupole...
steps.instruction: Use a mass spectrometer triple quadrupole which enables product and MRM (multiple reaction monitoring) scans. The methods were optimized for an Ultra-High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (UHPLC) coupled online to a mass spectrometer QQQ (triple quadrupole).

steps.title: Chromatographic separation is performed by reverse...
steps.instruction: Chromatographic separation is performed by reverse phase columns, such as an analytical Zorbax Eclipse Plus C18 (1.8 μm, 2.1 x 50mm) and a guard column Zorbax SB-C18, 1.8 μm.

steps.title: The ionization method used in the...
steps.instruction: The ionization method used in the mass spectrometry was an ESI (Electrospray Ionization) under the conditions: gas temperature of 300ºC, nitrogen flow rate of 10L min­ -1 , nebulizer pressure of 35psi and capillary voltage of 4000V. The mass spectrometer is operated by positive or negative mode according to method for phytohormone detection.

steps.title: INSERIR UMA TABELA OU COMO FOTO...
steps.instruction: INSERIR UMA TABELA OU COMO FOTO DO SETUP DO QQQ OU IDICANTO OS SEGMENTOS E O USO DO MRM DYNAMIC!!!

steps.title: Inject all sample randomly using the...
steps.instruction: Inject all sample randomly using the MRM method, which was used to generate the standard curves. Use the raw data to analyze mass spectra using Skyline software (section 3.3; step 6).

steps.title: For each compound, use the XICs...
steps.instruction: For each compound, use the XICs area exported to Microsoft Excel and convert to ng/mL using the standard linear curves (section 3.3; step 7). Quantification of ABA for a Sample 1 with a XIC area of

steps.title: ABA ; transition 263 >153; retention time 8
steps.instruction: ABA ; transition 263 >153; retention time 8.2 (Table 1). Conversion to ng/mL: Standard curve for ABA: y=137.64x + 993.19; where x is the sample concentration (ng/mL) and y is the XIC area (arbitrary units) ABA concentration in the sample

steps.title: 38.91ng/mL Conversion of ng/mL to ng/g of fresh tissue
steps.instruction: 38.91ng/mL Conversion of ng/mL to ng/g of fresh tissue: 38.91ng ---- 1000ul x------ 800ul Then: 31.13 ng of ABA from 110mg of fresh tissue, 282.98 ng/g fresh tissue.

steps.title: The authors would like to thank...

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Tutorial for analysis of mass spectra from small molecules by skyline software. Adapted from tutorial “ Skyline Small Molecule Targets” [ https://skyline.ms/_webdav/home/software/Skyline/@files/tutorials/SmallMolecule-3_6.pdf ] Install the Skyline Package (32 or 64 bits): https://skyline.ms/wiki/home/software/Skyline/page.view?name=SkylineInstall_64_4-1&submit=false or https://skyline.ms/project/home/software/Skyline/begin.view Generate a transition list table in accordance with the MRM parameters such as in the Table 1. Open the transition list file in the OpenOffice software (Avoid language incompatibly). Select and Copy all lines, except the column heading. Open Skyline package and click in blank document Proceed edit >>>> insert >>>> transition list Click in “ columns ” to edit . Select in accordance with the transition list create before . Use the mouse to dragging and to change the column order. Paste the transition list information. Click in check for errors Open Setting >>> transitions setting and configur e to processing the raw data from the QqQ mass spectrometry Open Setting >>> Save Current . Open File >> import >> Results Click in Ok to import and s ave the Project as myprojectname . Sky In the windown import Results >> click in OK !! Select and import all data from files .d, generated by LC/MS QqQ for all the samples. After the data uploading , open View >>>> retention times >>> and select Replicate Comparison Open View >>>> Peak Areas >>> and select replicate comparison. Proceed a double-click in the tabs “Peak Areas” and “Retention Times” . Click over the retention time (RT) bar to edit and correct the selected chromatogram area that was generated automatically. The dashed line could be moved!! Repeat for all compound and samples!!! Save the project. Open File >>> Export >> report >> select transition result >>>> OK , to export the peak areas of the all XICs as spreadsheet file. Open this file in the OpenOffice and use the XIC area for each compounds to obtain the quantitative information.
